rules.condition: [Patch rule — basal_cell_carcinoma_vs_benign_keratosis] Central pigmented area contains coarse white-to-cream granular inclusions (milia-like cysts or comedo-like openings) embedded within brownish-violaceous pigment; Peripheral zone shows scattered brown dots/globules or 'moth-eaten' edge fading gradually into surrounding skin without a sharp border; Color palette is mixed brown, gray-brown, and dull violaceous — NOT pearly white or translucent; No arborizing (tree-like) telangiectasias visible anywhere in the lesion; No blue-gray ovoid nests or spoke-wheel structures present; Lesion surface appears somewhat dull and keratotic rather than shiny or translucent
rules.action: Classify as Benign Keratosis. Confidence: high. Rule: When a lesion shows a central zone of mixed brown-to-violaceous pigmentation with a 'moth-eaten' or finely granular, milia-like white clod texture interspersed within the pigmented area, surrounded by a peripheral halo of scattered brown dots/globules fading into normal skin without arborizing vessels or blue-gray ovoid nests, classify as Benign Keratosis.

lineage.reason: patch rule for basal_cell_carcinoma_vs_benign_keratosis triggered by basal_cell_carcinoma_vs_benign_keratosis_ISIC_0024336

rules.condition: [Patch rule — basal_cell_carcinoma_vs_benign_keratosis] Lesion is small and relatively flat with ill-defined, irregular borders; Pigmentation consists of loosely aggregated brown/dark-brown granules, dots, and clods in a clustered but disorganized arrangement; Background skin is diffusely pink/erythematous with visible terminal hairs passing through or around the lesion; Absence of arborizing (telangiectatic) vessels, which are hallmark of BCC; Absence of pearly, translucent, or shiny white areas; Absence of blue-gray ovoid nests or multiple blue-gray globules; Absence of ulceration or erosion; Pigment pattern is mottled/moth-eaten rather than structurally organized (no clear pigment network, streaks, or spoke-wheel areas)
rules.action: Classify as Benign Keratosis. Confidence: medium. Rule: When a small, ill-defined pigmented lesion on hairy skin shows a disorganized cluster of brown-to-dark granules and dots WITHOUT arborizing vessels, without pearly/translucent areas, and without ulceration or blue-gray ovoid nests, set against a diffuse pink background with prominent terminal hairs, classify as Benign Keratosis.
